item.title: 新加坡專制vs中共專制 Singaporean vs. CCP|#我的學習筆記 #534 ‪‪‪‪‪‪‪‪‪‪‪@mynotebooks
item.itunes:title: 新加坡專制vs中共專制 Singaporean vs. CCP|#我的學習筆記 #534 ‪‪‪‪‪‪‪‪‪‪‪@mynotebooks
item.itunes:author: 我的學習筆記
item.itunes:summary:
Hello! Hello everyone! I'm Brother Aniu. Speaking of human rights organization Freedom House. "Global Freedom Report 2024" released. We, the Republic of China, scored a total of 94 points. Compared with Japan which scored 96 points. South Korea with 83 points. remain a free country. Singapore with 48 points. Rated as a Partially Free Country. The CCP only has 9 points. Less than 1/10 of ours. 3 points with North Korea. Classified as an unfree country again. Although mature democracies. overall economic performance. would be better than authoritarian countries. But a few authoritarian countries. will focus on economic growth. They are developing. beyond emerging democracies. For example, South Korea under Park Chung-hee. The CCP under Deng Xiaoping and. Singapore under Lee Kuan Yew and the like. South Korea has successfully achieved democratic transition. became a democratic country. And partially free Singapore. Still implement authoritarian system. Then it has nothing to do with the authoritarian rule. Of the Chinese Communist Party. What's the difference? Today we will do a comparison. Let’s talk about Singapore first. Talking about Singapore’s independence and founding of the country. to what it is today. It depends on the beginning. The Singapore model constructed by the rulers. The core concepts of this model emphasize. Because the country is small, lacks resources,. And has a fragile foundation for survival,. Faced with various threats all the time. That’s why you need to be strong. And the elite big government comes to coordinate. and make good use of resources. if it were not so. Society will be divided and turbulent. The Singapore model actually often sparks controversy. From the perspective of those who celebrate the Singapore model. Under the leadership of strongman Lee Kuan Yew. Elite government can strengthen politics. And encourage governance. So although Singapore is a small country. But it still has a high international reputation and influence. The people live a prosperous life and everyone lives. And works in peace and contentment. The Singapore model is a model of good governance. Considering the welfare of the people. Also known as benevolent authoritarianism. But those who hate the Singapore model think. Singapore pursues paternalistic governance. Social freedoms are trampled on. The will of the people is not respected. So I think the Singapore model is not advisable. Parent Status in Singapore Government. Not unchallenged at all. The public does not fully accept. This is also why Singapore is rated as. Some reasons for free countries. Some people also think. Lee Kuan Yew's Singapore People's Action Party. The reason why one party can be in power. For a long time and be dominant. The first thing to rely on is not the government. authoritative power. Rather, it can persuade the public to accept. Paternalistic governance by the ruling party. It is the ruling party that can show the people.

Material life continues to improve. Lee Kuan Yew in "Lee Kuan Yew's Key to Statecraft" This book claims. People don’t want democracy. They just want a house. Have medical conditions and have a job. There is a school where you can study. Lee Kuan Yew met these needs. shortly after World War II. Singapore was granted self-government. 1959. Lee Kuan Yew's People's Action Party recognized. Housing issues need urgent attention. in his campaign. Promise after winning the election. Provide low-cost housing to citizens. after winning the election. Brother Li took action immediately. established a vast public housing system. At present, about 80% of Singaporeans still. Live here now. The people take it from the government. Purchased a public housing flat. This is what we commonly call social housing. Solved the housing shortage problem. The government has also achieved. Home Ownership Commitment. Since 1960. The early days of building HDB flats. About 23% of the population lives in HDB flats. It will increase to 80% in 20 years. By 2021. Still more than 80% of the population. Living in a HDB flat. 18% down payment for HDB flat purchase. It’s a subsidy from the government. Directly from forced savings. Debited from provident fund account. It feels like Singaporeans buying a house. It’s like you don’t have to take out money. This is also Singapore’s “home ownership scheme”. reasons for implementation. Singapore’s public housing policy. Countries around the world are also rushing to learn from. It also brings benefits to the people of Singapore. Agree with this Singapore model. Since the founding of Singapore in 1965. It follows the education from the British colonial period. A bilingual education model is used. All students in Singapore. In addition to using English as the main teaching. You must also take courses in the mother tongue. Of your ethnic group. 2021. Singapore’s fiscal budget. Education expenditure: S$13.6 billion. Approximately 4% of GDP. This is also the case among ASEAN countries. Basic education level in Singapore. Important factors to maintain first place. education in singapore. Can be divided into 3 to 4 stages. The first stage is 6 years of primary school education. This is mandatory. The second stage is a 4 to 6 year secondary school course. If you reach the target, you can do it in the third stage. Choose a 3- to 5-year polytechnic. Or attend 2 years of junior college. Didn't reach target. Can study at Institute of Technical Education. You can also choose to go directly into social work. According to academic performance. polytechnic students. Those who have passed all subjects after graduation. You can get an associate's degree. About 1/5 can continue to study.

Bachelor's degree from a government university. Junior college or undergraduate preparatory students. then more than 60%. Can obtain admission to study. Diploma in Government. In Singapore’s education system, there is the same. It may be unacceptable to Taiwanese people. That’s from elementary and middle school to today. The principal or dean of students is also allowed to. With parental consent. Using caning to punish students. Although this is rarely implemented, it is. But still to other students. Acted as a deterrent. Bilingual education policy is the PAP government's. Based on national development and ethnic unity. inevitable choice. English has not only become a local. Strongly dominant working language and cross-ethnic language. It has also become a bridge for Singapore. Io integrate into the world. Make Singaporeans more competitive than neighboring countries. According to Singapore’s Ministry of Manpower. The "Labor Market Report" released in March this year. December 2023. Singapore’s total employed population. Reached 3.713,400 people. one third of them. Be a non-resident employee holding a work permit. These non-resident employees. Mainly concentrated in the construction and manufacturing industries. past year. Singapore's unemployment rate remains low. The long-term unemployment rate remains at 0.7%. Among them 30 years old and below. and those aged 50 to 59 years old. The long-term unemployment rates are 1% and 0.9% respectively. Singapore’s Ministry of Manpower stated. As the Star Nation’s fertility rate declines. Employment is unlikely to sustain high growth. The construction industry will continue to flourish in the future. Expected employment growth. mainly from non-residents. However, the Singapore government’s policy. On the resident labor force. Not just focusing on whether you can get a job. It’s about whether you can earn a high salary. Find a job in a high-growth field. Singapore recently introduced a four-day work week. That is, 3 days off per week. It is planned to start from December 1 this year. Employees can submit to their employer. Four day week job application. Increase telecommuting days. Or adjust working hours and other flexible working requirements. In line with the international trend of retaining talents. Singapore will also be the first in Asia. Countries with four-day working week. Singapore has the largest aging population in the world. one of the fastest countries. But Singapore’s pension system. Ranked 7th in the global ranking. Ranked first in Asia for 15 consecutive years. The Singapore government uses the "provident fund system" to. To protect the elderly. The financial burden of life after retirement. This provident fund is actually. Commonly known as pension pension. is a compulsory savings plan. All citizens and permanent residents. Just start working. A certain percentage of wages must be allocated. Go to this provident fund account. Employees under 55 years old. 20% self-withdrawal. Employer pays 17%.

Contribution ratio. It is decided based on age. For all Singapore employees. All adopt the same standard. Such a system. Let all the silver-haired people. You will be protected after retirement. Singapore’s Provident Fund Operation Model. Whether it is asset allocation. Or investment effect. are relatively diverse and stable. To ensure that the provident fund system can continue. The Singaporean government has also shown determination. Extend the retirement age year by year. Protecting the employment rights of senior citizens. It also eases social labor, medical. financial pressure etc. Mandatory savings and mandatory postponement of retirement age. This is difficult to do in a democratic country. And actually Singapore. It's not as good as it seems. After all, you still probably. Because if you evaluate the government, you will lose all benefits. As for the above benefits. In an authoritarian country? Surely it can be done, right? So has the CCP done it? Next, let’s talk about the occupied areas. Talking about Zhou Jinbo, Representative. Of the National People’s Congress. In this year’s two sessions. Recommended for 65 year olds. Establish a "universal basic pension system" I hope it can make people who have never worked. Have not paid social security. Elderly people who cannot enjoy basic pension security. can meet basic life needs. This may be the case in previous National People’s Congresses. The most valuable proposal. It has also become a hot topic in society. And Li Qiang, the Prime Minister of the Communist Party of China. In the government work report on March 5. "Urban and Rural Residents Basic Pension" The monthly minimum standard is increased by 20 yuan." There are now 170 million elderly people in occupied areas. Receive pension insurance for urban and rural residents. From 103 yuan per month. Increased to 123 yuan per month. The CCP is not claiming. Are we an inseparable piece? So can we lead both sides? The CCP’s pension system is a “dual-track system”. Within the system and outside the system. Civil servants and public institutions within the system. Participate in the pension insurance of government agencies. And institutions. No need to pay yourself. Pension is roughly. Linked to retirement salary. Those outside the system are corporate employees. It is a combination of social coordination and personal accounts. That is, companies and individuals. Sharing pension insurance premiums. But more migrant workers. and farmers are not covered in this set. In the pension security system. But for the sake of good-looking data. The CCP authorities have also targeted this group of people. Disburse basic pension to urban and rural residents. This is Prime Minister Li Qiang. The 170 million elderly people. Who increased their income by 20 yuan. The greater effect of this policy is. It allows the CCP to declare to the outside world. Pension security covers 96.8%. In January 2023.

There are 2.6 million job seekers including graduates. Apply for 37,100 entry-level government jobs. Among people aged 16 to 24 in occupied areas. Nearly 1 in 5 is unemployed. Alibaba, Tencent and other technology companies. have carried out large-scale layoffs. From August 2023. As the unemployment rate hits record highs. China stops publishing youth unemployment data. This has also led to Internet companies. blatant violation of the constitution. Launch of 996 work system. 9 a.m. every day. Work until 9pm. Work 6 days a week. Alibaba founder Jack Ma said. "It's a huge blessing to be able to do 996. Many companies, many people. Even if you want 996, there is no chance.”. I don’t know if he said anything against it. compared to singapore. Hope to be the first in Asia. Countries with 4-day working week. The CCP has become the world’s first. A place with a 12-hour work day. Sure enough, once again we are far ahead. Compulsory education in occupied areas. It is 6 years of elementary school plus 3 years of junior high school. No more than 50% from junior high school to high school. These students. Will attend vocational and technical schools. Less than half of the remaining students. Entering 3 years of high school studies. Enter university by taking the National College Entrance Examination. Admission to universities in occupied areas. It is not a "nationally consistent" scoring standard. but based on student. Ranking of "College Entrance Examination" scores. In each province. Some students in educational resources. Study in a relatively high province. But I will do it so that it will be easier for me. To get into a prestigious school. So I tried every means. Go to a province with lower admission standards. Go take the college entrance examination. Exploiting the Loophole of “Provincial Differences”. The same score in different provinces. there is a huge difference. Last year, Xi'an City, Shaanxi Province. There are over ten thousand people. Protest gathered at the Provincial Department of Education. Shouting "Xi'an babies want to go to school" The cause is Xi'an City. In the high school entrance examination. A large number of students from Henan appear. Come to Xi'an to grab a spot. As a result, many local students in Xi'an failed to pass the exam. Parents of these Xi'an students took to the streets. Questioning the exam qualifications of these Henan students. Protest against entrance examination methods and systems. create artificial inequalities. In addition to regional inequality. There is also status inequality. Studying in college in occupied areas. It’s not about taking the college entrance examination. As long as the score meets the standard. Many universities allocate quotas. Even if the score is up to standard. Even if your identity and qualifications are not met,. You will still be rejected. In a communist society. "Class inheritance" always exists. The Communist Party has always emphasized. I am a hero and a good man. I'm a reactionary bastard.

In such a "hereditary" society. Some red second and third generation people. With your dad or your grandpa. You can get into Peking University with low scores. Study in these famous schools like Tsinghua University. Different from bilingual education in Singapore. The occupied areas are being de-Englishized. 2023. Xi'an Jiaotong University issued a notice. He expressed that he would no longer submit the results. Of CET-4 and CET-6. Linked to undergraduate graduation and bachelor's degree. Back in 2020. Education Department of the Communist Party of China. It is prohibited for elementary and junior high schools. Use textbooks from overseas. A government adviser suggested. English should be stopped in the college entrance examination. Some professors who did not want to be named said. Universities also do not encourage. Original English and translated books. especially in the news. and more sensitive subjects such as constitutional studies. Communist Party’s Orthodox Theory. is replacing english. Elementary, middle and high school. Being asked to read. Xi Jinping’s new era. New textbooks on socialist thought with Chinese characteristics. Every student is required to. Attend a class once a week. Unlike before, everyone had a copy of Mao’s Quotations. Different approaches but similar results. Finally we talk about. Housing development in occupied areas. before reform and opening up. The occupied areas implement unit-based. Welfare housing allocation system. The government is fully responsible for. Responsibility for housing urban residents. The central government formulates plans to allocate funds. Local governments and units use this money. Build housing and distribute it to employees. housing benefit distribution system. resulting in a lack of housing and small living space. Housing is of poor quality and lacks maintenance. Issues such as unfair housing distribution. before housing reform. Urban housing area per capita. Only 3.8 square meters. That’s more than 1 square meter for us. About the same size as your toilet. There is simply no way to meet everyone’s housing needs. from the 20th century. Beginning in the late 1970s. The CCP implements housing marketization. Socialization and Monetization Reform. After more than 20 years of housing system reform,. There are still many problems in the housing sector. Take Peking as an example. In 2000. Price per square meter. About 2800 RMB. In 2020. It has risen to. 58,000 per square meter. Complete marketization. Can reap economic dividends. There are only powerful people and financial groups. After all, people who have money to buy a house. Mostly this group of people. It turned out that ordinary families who could not afford a house. It will be even harder to buy a house. Supporting affordable housing. That is our national residence in Taiwan. Mainly to protect grassroots officials.

But will their lives be difficult? Low-rent housing is provided by the government. Room type at 1/3 the price. Its basic condition is that citizens. But you still need to have some connections with the government. At least there's someone at home. You can only get it in the civil service system. Workers and migrant workers whose lives are more difficult. They are never guaranteed. This kind of housing system. For families with real housing difficulties. causing greater injustice. These officials and civil servants. Rent a house from the government. The phenomenon of subletting to migrant workers with extra money. This is also common in occupied areas. However, the government has always implemented this policy. viewed as socialist benefits. Give it to the system. No corrupt grassroots civil servants. so that these people can enjoy. The so-called dividends of communism. Singapore and China. Both are authoritarian systems. But what Singapore implements is. benevolent authoritarianism. More like a perfect communist system. assumes the responsibility of comprehensively. Taking care of the people. Education, medical care, residence, elderly care. Responsibilities required in all aspects of life. but. Of course, benevolent despotism is also despotism. Also has fatal flaws. The first is that benevolent dictators are not common. The second is that there is no guarantee that. They will always do good things. Third, it is difficult to guarantee their heirs. will have the same ability to continue doing good deeds. What the CCP implements is. socialism with Chinese characteristics. Socialism can only be implemented within the party. They divided society into three, six, and nine grades. Leaders, officials, citizens, farmers. Distribute social resources from most to least. More focus on protection. The privileged class of leaders and officials. basic social security. It is difficult to cover many farmers. Li Keqiang before his death. I once said at a press conference. China has 600 million low- and middle-income people. The monthly income is only about 1,000 yuan. There are also 67 million people living in extreme poverty. Because today’s Holy. Talk about eradicating poverty. Therefore, it was not included in the report. It’s different from a democratic country. The well-being of people in authoritarian countries. completely in the hands of the head of state. And in a democratic country. People's well-being may be slow to improve. But if an official or political party does something wrong. People can use their votes to replace them. Make sure the government is on the right path. However, authoritarian countries can only use. The bloody way of revolution has changed. alright. So that’s it for this episode. if you like. Welcome to click subscribe and share it for us. If you have capable friends. You can also join our membership. Or click Super Thank You. In addition, our DC group is now open. Everyone is welcome to come in and chat.

My study notes. See you next time. ByeBye.

====

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wtlFgc9O-OY

雖然成熟的民主國家

在經濟上的整體表現

會優於專制國家

但少數的專制國家

會把注意力集中在經濟增長上面

它們在發展方面

超越了新興的民主國家

例如朴正熙領導下的韓國

鄧小平領導下的中共

以及李光耀領導下的新加坡之類的

新加坡跟中共

一樣都是威權體制

但是有什麼不一樣呢？

今天就我們來聊聊新加坡跟中共的專制有什麼不同吧！

#臺灣 #台灣 #中華民國 #中國 #新加坡 #專制 #中共 #南韓 #專制政府 #民主 #國家 #體制 #李光耀 #李明龍 #溫家寶 #習近平 #江澤明 #中共專制 #新加坡專制 #民生 #政策 #國策

📍讓我們繼續活下去 ► http://bit.ly/2tGL3eB

LINE貼圖搜尋「我的學習筆記」或是點下方連結

我的學習筆記2

https://reurl.cc/qkk38y

我的學習筆記3

https://reurl.cc/n77Rn6

我的學習筆記4

https://reurl.cc/mllNq9

🎬更多的學習筆記

YOUTUBE長影片討論 ► https://reurl.cc/gy2ZR

Tiktok上更多短影片 ► https://reurl.cc/bkjjz3

Facebook關注更多消息 ► https://bit.ly/2TFDZXC

Instagram掌握國際大事 ► https://reurl.cc/GyxEp

方格子學術公眾化成果 ► http://bit.ly/32J6mrK

DisCord群組一起聊天抬槓►https://discord.gg/YmSkbSVrUC

小額支付贊助我歐付寶►http://bit.ly/2Z1s1yA

當然還有PayPal►PayPal.Me/wMyNotebook

😉特別感謝6月份支持學習筆記的朋友

Polya Yang、柯誠譽、Rosemary 、阿里不打、Sion Ein、Roots Ko、Wu Pin-Ying、赤炎、懶虎提姆閣、Roy Li、KaHu Lau、葉小雲、李文傑、陳建宏、Louis Tseng、薩麥爾_MLBB、祈翠、Mariposa Bay、語禪、Krib Stek、張輔庭、whiskyhot886 、Chen Chong Lee、Charlie曾、蔡岳峰、Chung-Ming Wang、上善若水、林雋凱

📍特別感謝每位默默支持我們的朋友們，你們給的不只是錢財，更是一段友誼 當你像小蝦米一樣對抗大鯨魚，當你的敵人遠比你想像中強大的時候 發現了這個世界，其實有一群未曾相識卻力挺你的朋友，我想，這就是世界上最美好的感覺了
item.description:
Hello! Hello everyone! I'm Brother Aniu. Speaking of human rights organization Freedom House. "Global Freedom Report 2024" released. We, the Republic of China, scored a total of 94 points. Compared with Japan which scored 96 points. South Korea with 83 points. remain a free country. Singapore with 48 points. Rated as a Partially Free Country. The CCP only has 9 points. Less than 1/10 of ours. 3 points with North Korea. Classified as an unfree country again. Although mature democracies. overall economic performance. would be better than authoritarian countries. But a few authoritarian countries. will focus on economic growth. They are developing. beyond emerging democracies. For example, South Korea under Park Chung-hee. The CCP under Deng Xiaoping and. Singapore under Lee Kuan Yew and the like. South Korea has successfully achieved democratic transition. became a democratic country. And partially free Singapore. Still implement authoritarian system. Then it has nothing to do with the authoritarian rule. Of the Chinese Communist Party. What's the difference? Today we will do a comparison. Let’s talk about Singapore first. Talking about Singapore’s independence and founding of the country. to what it is today. It depends on the beginning. The Singapore model constructed by the rulers. The core concepts of this model emphasize. Because the country is small, lacks resources,. And has a fragile foundation for survival,. Faced with various threats all the time. That’s why you need to be strong. And the elite big government comes to coordinate. and make good use of resources. if it were not so. Society will be divided and turbulent. The Singapore model actually often sparks controversy. From the perspective of those who celebrate the Singapore model. Under the leadership of strongman Lee Kuan Yew. Elite government can strengthen politics. And encourage governance. So although Singapore is a small country. But it still has a high international reputation and influence. The people live a prosperous life and everyone lives. And works in peace and contentment. The Singapore model is a model of good governance. Considering the welfare of the people. Also known as benevolent authoritarianism. But those who hate the Singapore model think. Singapore pursues paternalistic governance. Social freedoms are trampled on. The will of the people is not respected. So I think the Singapore model is not advisable. Parent Status in Singapore Government. Not unchallenged at all. The public does not fully accept. This is also why Singapore is rated as. Some reasons for free countries. Some people also think. Lee Kuan Yew's Singapore People's Action Party. The reason why one party can be in power. For a long time and be dominant. The first thing to rely on is not the government. authoritative power. Rather, it can persuade the public to accept. Paternalistic governance by the ruling party. It is the ruling party that can show the people.

Material life continues to improve. Lee Kuan Yew in "Lee Kuan Yew's Key to Statecraft" This book claims. People don’t want democracy. They just want a house. Have medical conditions and have a job. There is a school where you can study. Lee Kuan Yew met these needs. shortly after World War II. Singapore was granted self-government. 1959. Lee Kuan Yew's People's Action Party recognized. Housing issues need urgent attention. in his campaign. Promise after winning the election. Provide low-cost housing to citizens. after winning the election. Brother Li took action immediately. established a vast public housing system. At present, about 80% of Singaporeans still. Live here now. The people take it from the government. Purchased a public housing flat. This is what we commonly call social housing. Solved the housing shortage problem. The government has also achieved. Home Ownership Commitment. Since 1960. The early days of building HDB flats. About 23% of the population lives in HDB flats. It will increase to 80% in 20 years. By 2021. Still more than 80% of the population. Living in a HDB flat. 18% down payment for HDB flat purchase. It’s a subsidy from the government. Directly from forced savings. Debited from provident fund account. It feels like Singaporeans buying a house. It’s like you don’t have to take out money. This is also Singapore’s “home ownership scheme”. reasons for implementation. Singapore’s public housing policy. Countries around the world are also rushing to learn from. It also brings benefits to the people of Singapore. Agree with this Singapore model. Since the founding of Singapore in 1965. It follows the education from the British colonial period. A bilingual education model is used. All students in Singapore. In addition to using English as the main teaching. You must also take courses in the mother tongue. Of your ethnic group. 2021. Singapore’s fiscal budget. Education expenditure: S$13.6 billion. Approximately 4% of GDP. This is also the case among ASEAN countries. Basic education level in Singapore. Important factors to maintain first place. education in singapore. Can be divided into 3 to 4 stages. The first stage is 6 years of primary school education. This is mandatory. The second stage is a 4 to 6 year secondary school course. If you reach the target, you can do it in the third stage. Choose a 3- to 5-year polytechnic. Or attend 2 years of junior college. Didn't reach target. Can study at Institute of Technical Education. You can also choose to go directly into social work. According to academic performance. polytechnic students. Those who have passed all subjects after graduation. You can get an associate's degree. About 1/5 can continue to study.

Bachelor's degree from a government university. Junior college or undergraduate preparatory students. then more than 60%. Can obtain admission to study. Diploma in Government. In Singapore’s education system, there is the same. It may be unacceptable to Taiwanese people. That’s from elementary and middle school to today. The principal or dean of students is also allowed to. With parental consent. Using caning to punish students. Although this is rarely implemented, it is. But still to other students. Acted as a deterrent. Bilingual education policy is the PAP government's. Based on national development and ethnic unity. inevitable choice. English has not only become a local. Strongly dominant working language and cross-ethnic language. It has also become a bridge for Singapore. Io integrate into the world. Make Singaporeans more competitive than neighboring countries. According to Singapore’s Ministry of Manpower. The "Labor Market Report" released in March this year. December 2023. Singapore’s total employed population. Reached 3.713,400 people. one third of them. Be a non-resident employee holding a work permit. These non-resident employees. Mainly concentrated in the construction and manufacturing industries. past year. Singapore's unemployment rate remains low. The long-term unemployment rate remains at 0.7%. Among them 30 years old and below. and those aged 50 to 59 years old. The long-term unemployment rates are 1% and 0.9% respectively. Singapore’s Ministry of Manpower stated. As the Star Nation’s fertility rate declines. Employment is unlikely to sustain high growth. The construction industry will continue to flourish in the future. Expected employment growth. mainly from non-residents. However, the Singapore government’s policy. On the resident labor force. Not just focusing on whether you can get a job. It’s about whether you can earn a high salary. Find a job in a high-growth field. Singapore recently introduced a four-day work week. That is, 3 days off per week. It is planned to start from December 1 this year. Employees can submit to their employer. Four day week job application. Increase telecommuting days. Or adjust working hours and other flexible working requirements. In line with the international trend of retaining talents. Singapore will also be the first in Asia. Countries with four-day working week. Singapore has the largest aging population in the world. one of the fastest countries. But Singapore’s pension system. Ranked 7th in the global ranking. Ranked first in Asia for 15 consecutive years. The Singapore government uses the "provident fund system" to. To protect the elderly. The financial burden of life after retirement. This provident fund is actually. Commonly known as pension pension. is a compulsory savings plan. All citizens and permanent residents. Just start working. A certain percentage of wages must be allocated. Go to this provident fund account. Employees under 55 years old. 20% self-withdrawal. Employer pays 17%.

Contribution ratio. It is decided based on age. For all Singapore employees. All adopt the same standard. Such a system. Let all the silver-haired people. You will be protected after retirement. Singapore’s Provident Fund Operation Model. Whether it is asset allocation. Or investment effect. are relatively diverse and stable. To ensure that the provident fund system can continue. The Singaporean government has also shown determination. Extend the retirement age year by year. Protecting the employment rights of senior citizens. It also eases social labor, medical. financial pressure etc. Mandatory savings and mandatory postponement of retirement age. This is difficult to do in a democratic country. And actually Singapore. It's not as good as it seems. After all, you still probably. Because if you evaluate the government, you will lose all benefits. As for the above benefits. In an authoritarian country? Surely it can be done, right? So has the CCP done it? Next, let’s talk about the occupied areas. Talking about Zhou Jinbo, Representative. Of the National People’s Congress. In this year’s two sessions. Recommended for 65 year olds. Establish a "universal basic pension system" I hope it can make people who have never worked. Have not paid social security. Elderly people who cannot enjoy basic pension security. can meet basic life needs. This may be the case in previous National People’s Congresses. The most valuable proposal. It has also become a hot topic in society. And Li Qiang, the Prime Minister of the Communist Party of China. In the government work report on March 5. "Urban and Rural Residents Basic Pension" The monthly minimum standard is increased by 20 yuan." There are now 170 million elderly people in occupied areas. Receive pension insurance for urban and rural residents. From 103 yuan per month. Increased to 123 yuan per month. The CCP is not claiming. Are we an inseparable piece? So can we lead both sides? The CCP’s pension system is a “dual-track system”. Within the system and outside the system. Civil servants and public institutions within the system. Participate in the pension insurance of government agencies. And institutions. No need to pay yourself. Pension is roughly. Linked to retirement salary. Those outside the system are corporate employees. It is a combination of social coordination and personal accounts. That is, companies and individuals. Sharing pension insurance premiums. But more migrant workers. and farmers are not covered in this set. In the pension security system. But for the sake of good-looking data. The CCP authorities have also targeted this group of people. Disburse basic pension to urban and rural residents. This is Prime Minister Li Qiang. The 170 million elderly people. Who increased their income by 20 yuan. The greater effect of this policy is. It allows the CCP to declare to the outside world. Pension security covers 96.8%. In January 2023.

There are 2.6 million job seekers including graduates. Apply for 37,100 entry-level government jobs. Among people aged 16 to 24 in occupied areas. Nearly 1 in 5 is unemployed. Alibaba, Tencent and other technology companies. have carried out large-scale layoffs. From August 2023. As the unemployment rate hits record highs. China stops publishing youth unemployment data. This has also led to Internet companies. blatant violation of the constitution. Launch of 996 work system. 9 a.m. every day. Work until 9pm. Work 6 days a week. Alibaba founder Jack Ma said. "It's a huge blessing to be able to do 996. Many companies, many people. Even if you want 996, there is no chance.”. I don’t know if he said anything against it. compared to singapore. Hope to be the first in Asia. Countries with 4-day working week. The CCP has become the world’s first. A place with a 12-hour work day. Sure enough, once again we are far ahead. Compulsory education in occupied areas. It is 6 years of elementary school plus 3 years of junior high school. No more than 50% from junior high school to high school. These students. Will attend vocational and technical schools. Less than half of the remaining students. Entering 3 years of high school studies. Enter university by taking the National College Entrance Examination. Admission to universities in occupied areas. It is not a "nationally consistent" scoring standard. but based on student. Ranking of "College Entrance Examination" scores. In each province. Some students in educational resources. Study in a relatively high province. But I will do it so that it will be easier for me. To get into a prestigious school. So I tried every means. Go to a province with lower admission standards. Go take the college entrance examination. Exploiting the Loophole of “Provincial Differences”. The same score in different provinces. there is a huge difference. Last year, Xi'an City, Shaanxi Province. There are over ten thousand people. Protest gathered at the Provincial Department of Education. Shouting "Xi'an babies want to go to school" The cause is Xi'an City. In the high school entrance examination. A large number of students from Henan appear. Come to Xi'an to grab a spot. As a result, many local students in Xi'an failed to pass the exam. Parents of these Xi'an students took to the streets. Questioning the exam qualifications of these Henan students. Protest against entrance examination methods and systems. create artificial inequalities. In addition to regional inequality. There is also status inequality. Studying in college in occupied areas. It’s not about taking the college entrance examination. As long as the score meets the standard. Many universities allocate quotas. Even if the score is up to standard. Even if your identity and qualifications are not met,. You will still be rejected. In a communist society. "Class inheritance" always exists. The Communist Party has always emphasized. I am a hero and a good man. I'm a reactionary bastard.

In such a "hereditary" society. Some red second and third generation people. With your dad or your grandpa. You can get into Peking University with low scores. Study in these famous schools like Tsinghua University. Different from bilingual education in Singapore. The occupied areas are being de-Englishized. 2023. Xi'an Jiaotong University issued a notice. He expressed that he would no longer submit the results. Of CET-4 and CET-6. Linked to undergraduate graduation and bachelor's degree. Back in 2020. Education Department of the Communist Party of China. It is prohibited for elementary and junior high schools. Use textbooks from overseas. A government adviser suggested. English should be stopped in the college entrance examination. Some professors who did not want to be named said. Universities also do not encourage. Original English and translated books. especially in the news. and more sensitive subjects such as constitutional studies. Communist Party’s Orthodox Theory. is replacing english. Elementary, middle and high school. Being asked to read. Xi Jinping’s new era. New textbooks on socialist thought with Chinese characteristics. Every student is required to. Attend a class once a week. Unlike before, everyone had a copy of Mao’s Quotations. Different approaches but similar results. Finally we talk about. Housing development in occupied areas. before reform and opening up. The occupied areas implement unit-based. Welfare housing allocation system. The government is fully responsible for. Responsibility for housing urban residents. The central government formulates plans to allocate funds. Local governments and units use this money. Build housing and distribute it to employees. housing benefit distribution system. resulting in a lack of housing and small living space. Housing is of poor quality and lacks maintenance. Issues such as unfair housing distribution. before housing reform. Urban housing area per capita. Only 3.8 square meters. That’s more than 1 square meter for us. About the same size as your toilet. There is simply no way to meet everyone’s housing needs. from the 20th century. Beginning in the late 1970s. The CCP implements housing marketization. Socialization and Monetization Reform. After more than 20 years of housing system reform,. There are still many problems in the housing sector. Take Peking as an example. In 2000. Price per square meter. About 2800 RMB. In 2020. It has risen to. 58,000 per square meter. Complete marketization. Can reap economic dividends. There are only powerful people and financial groups. After all, people who have money to buy a house. Mostly this group of people. It turned out that ordinary families who could not afford a house. It will be even harder to buy a house. Supporting affordable housing. That is our national residence in Taiwan. Mainly to protect grassroots officials.

But will their lives be difficult? Low-rent housing is provided by the government. Room type at 1/3 the price. Its basic condition is that citizens. But you still need to have some connections with the government. At least there's someone at home. You can only get it in the civil service system. Workers and migrant workers whose lives are more difficult. They are never guaranteed. This kind of housing system. For families with real housing difficulties. causing greater injustice. These officials and civil servants. Rent a house from the government. The phenomenon of subletting to migrant workers with extra money. This is also common in occupied areas. However, the government has always implemented this policy. viewed as socialist benefits. Give it to the system. No corrupt grassroots civil servants. so that these people can enjoy. The so-called dividends of communism. Singapore and China. Both are authoritarian systems. But what Singapore implements is. benevolent authoritarianism. More like a perfect communist system. assumes the responsibility of comprehensively. Taking care of the people. Education, medical care, residence, elderly care. Responsibilities required in all aspects of life. but. Of course, benevolent despotism is also despotism. Also has fatal flaws. The first is that benevolent dictators are not common. The second is that there is no guarantee that. They will always do good things. Third, it is difficult to guarantee their heirs. will have the same ability to continue doing good deeds. What the CCP implements is. socialism with Chinese characteristics. Socialism can only be implemented within the party. They divided society into three, six, and nine grades. Leaders, officials, citizens, farmers. Distribute social resources from most to least. More focus on protection. The privileged class of leaders and officials. basic social security. It is difficult to cover many farmers. Li Keqiang before his death. I once said at a press conference. China has 600 million low- and middle-income people. The monthly income is only about 1,000 yuan. There are also 67 million people living in extreme poverty. Because today’s Holy. Talk about eradicating poverty. Therefore, it was not included in the report. It’s different from a democratic country. The well-being of people in authoritarian countries. completely in the hands of the head of state. And in a democratic country. People's well-being may be slow to improve. But if an official or political party does something wrong. People can use their votes to replace them. Make sure the government is on the right path. However, authoritarian countries can only use. The bloody way of revolution has changed. alright. So that’s it for this episode. if you like. Welcome to click subscribe and share it for us. If you have capable friends. You can also join our membership. Or click Super Thank You. In addition, our DC group is now open. Everyone is welcome to come in and chat.

My study notes. See you next time. ByeBye.

====

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wtlFgc9O-OY

雖然成熟的民主國家

在經濟上的整體表現

會優於專制國家

但少數的專制國家

會把注意力集中在經濟增長上面

它們在發展方面

超越了新興的民主國家

例如朴正熙領導下的韓國

鄧小平領導下的中共

以及李光耀領導下的新加坡之類的

新加坡跟中共

一樣都是威權體制

但是有什麼不一樣呢？

今天就我們來聊聊新加坡跟中共的專制有什麼不同吧！

#臺灣 #台灣 #中華民國 #中國 #新加坡 #專制 #中共 #南韓 #專制政府 #民主 #國家 #體制 #李光耀 #李明龍 #溫家寶 #習近平 #江澤明 #中共專制 #新加坡專制 #民生 #政策 #國策

📍讓我們繼續活下去 ► http://bit.ly/2tGL3eB

LINE貼圖搜尋「我的學習筆記」或是點下方連結

我的學習筆記2

https://reurl.cc/qkk38y

我的學習筆記3

https://reurl.cc/n77Rn6

我的學習筆記4

https://reurl.cc/mllNq9

🎬更多的學習筆記

YOUTUBE長影片討論 ► https://reurl.cc/gy2ZR

Tiktok上更多短影片 ► https://reurl.cc/bkjjz3

Facebook關注更多消息 ► https://bit.ly/2TFDZXC

Instagram掌握國際大事 ► https://reurl.cc/GyxEp

方格子學術公眾化成果 ► http://bit.ly/32J6mrK

DisCord群組一起聊天抬槓►https://discord.gg/YmSkbSVrUC

小額支付贊助我歐付寶►http://bit.ly/2Z1s1yA

當然還有PayPal►PayPal.Me/wMyNotebook

😉特別感謝6月份支持學習筆記的朋友

Polya Yang、柯誠譽、Rosemary 、阿里不打、Sion Ein、Roots Ko、Wu Pin-Ying、赤炎、懶虎提姆閣、Roy Li、KaHu Lau、葉小雲、李文傑、陳建宏、Louis Tseng、薩麥爾_MLBB、祈翠、Mariposa Bay、語禪、Krib Stek、張輔庭、whiskyhot886 、Chen Chong Lee、Charlie曾、蔡岳峰、Chung-Ming Wang、上善若水、林雋凱

📍特別感謝每位默默支持我們的朋友們，你們給的不只是錢財，更是一段友誼 當你像小蝦米一樣對抗大鯨魚，當你的敵人遠比你想像中強大的時候 發現了這個世界，其實有一群未曾相識卻力挺你的朋友，我想，這就是世界上最美好的感覺了
item.content:encoded:
Hello! Hello everyone! I'm Brother Aniu. Speaking of human rights organization Freedom House. "Global Freedom Report 2024" released. We, the Republic of China, scored a total of 94 points. Compared with Japan which scored 96 points. South Korea with 83 points. remain a free country. Singapore with 48 points. Rated as a Partially Free Country. The CCP only has 9 points. Less than 1/10 of ours. 3 points with North Korea. Classified as an unfree country again. Although mature democracies. overall economic performance. would be better than authoritarian countries. But a few authoritarian countries. will focus on economic growth. They are developing. beyond emerging democracies. For example, South Korea under Park Chung-hee. The CCP under Deng Xiaoping and. Singapore under Lee Kuan Yew and the like. South Korea has successfully achieved democratic transition. became a democratic country. And partially free Singapore. Still implement authoritarian system. Then it has nothing to do with the authoritarian rule. Of the Chinese Communist Party. What's the difference? Today we will do a comparison. Let’s talk about Singapore first. Talking about Singapore’s independence and founding of the country. to what it is today. It depends on the beginning. The Singapore model constructed by the rulers. The core concepts of this model emphasize. Because the country is small, lacks resources,. And has a fragile foundation for survival,. Faced with various threats all the time. That’s why you need to be strong. And the elite big government comes to coordinate. and make good use of resources. if it were not so. Society will be divided and turbulent. The Singapore model actually often sparks controversy. From the perspective of those who celebrate the Singapore model. Under the leadership of strongman Lee Kuan Yew. Elite government can strengthen politics. And encourage governance. So although Singapore is a small country. But it still has a high international reputation and influence. The people live a prosperous life and everyone lives. And works in peace and contentment. The Singapore model is a model of good governance. Considering the welfare of the people. Also known as benevolent authoritarianism. But those who hate the Singapore model think. Singapore pursues paternalistic governance. Social freedoms are trampled on. The will of the people is not respected. So I think the Singapore model is not advisable. Parent Status in Singapore Government. Not unchallenged at all. The public does not fully accept. This is also why Singapore is rated as. Some reasons for free countries. Some people also think. Lee Kuan Yew's Singapore People's Action Party. The reason why one party can be in power. For a long time and be dominant. The first thing to rely on is not the government. authoritative power. Rather, it can persuade the public to accept. Paternalistic governance by the ruling party. It is the ruling party that can show the people.

Material life continues to improve. Lee Kuan Yew in "Lee Kuan Yew's Key to Statecraft" This book claims. People don’t want democracy. They just want a house. Have medical conditions and have a job. There is a school where you can study. Lee Kuan Yew met these needs. shortly after World War II. Singapore was granted self-government. 1959. Lee Kuan Yew's People's Action Party recognized. Housing issues need urgent attention. in his campaign. Promise after winning the election. Provide low-cost housing to citizens. after winning the election. Brother Li took action immediately. established a vast public housing system. At present, about 80% of Singaporeans still. Live here now. The people take it from the government. Purchased a public housing flat. This is what we commonly call social housing. Solved the housing shortage problem. The government has also achieved. Home Ownership Commitment. Since 1960. The early days of building HDB flats. About 23% of the population lives in HDB flats. It will increase to 80% in 20 years. By 2021. Still more than 80% of the population. Living in a HDB flat. 18% down payment for HDB flat purchase. It’s a subsidy from the government. Directly from forced savings. Debited from provident fund account. It feels like Singaporeans buying a house. It’s like you don’t have to take out money. This is also Singapore’s “home ownership scheme”. reasons for implementation. Singapore’s public housing policy. Countries around the world are also rushing to learn from. It also brings benefits to the people of Singapore. Agree with this Singapore model. Since the founding of Singapore in 1965. It follows the education from the British colonial period. A bilingual education model is used. All students in Singapore. In addition to using English as the main teaching. You must also take courses in the mother tongue. Of your ethnic group. 2021. Singapore’s fiscal budget. Education expenditure: S$13.6 billion. Approximately 4% of GDP. This is also the case among ASEAN countries. Basic education level in Singapore. Important factors to maintain first place. education in singapore. Can be divided into 3 to 4 stages. The first stage is 6 years of primary school education. This is mandatory. The second stage is a 4 to 6 year secondary school course. If you reach the target, you can do it in the third stage. Choose a 3- to 5-year polytechnic. Or attend 2 years of junior college. Didn't reach target. Can study at Institute of Technical Education. You can also choose to go directly into social work. According to academic performance. polytechnic students. Those who have passed all subjects after graduation. You can get an associate's degree. About 1/5 can continue to study.

Bachelor's degree from a government university. Junior college or undergraduate preparatory students. then more than 60%. Can obtain admission to study. Diploma in Government. In Singapore’s education system, there is the same. It may be unacceptable to Taiwanese people. That’s from elementary and middle school to today. The principal or dean of students is also allowed to. With parental consent. Using caning to punish students. Although this is rarely implemented, it is. But still to other students. Acted as a deterrent. Bilingual education policy is the PAP government's. Based on national development and ethnic unity. inevitable choice. English has not only become a local. Strongly dominant working language and cross-ethnic language. It has also become a bridge for Singapore. Io integrate into the world. Make Singaporeans more competitive than neighboring countries. According to Singapore’s Ministry of Manpower. The "Labor Market Report" released in March this year. December 2023. Singapore’s total employed population. Reached 3.713,400 people. one third of them. Be a non-resident employee holding a work permit. These non-resident employees. Mainly concentrated in the construction and manufacturing industries. past year. Singapore's unemployment rate remains low. The long-term unemployment rate remains at 0.7%. Among them 30 years old and below. and those aged 50 to 59 years old. The long-term unemployment rates are 1% and 0.9% respectively. Singapore’s Ministry of Manpower stated. As the Star Nation’s fertility rate declines. Employment is unlikely to sustain high growth. The construction industry will continue to flourish in the future. Expected employment growth. mainly from non-residents. However, the Singapore government’s policy. On the resident labor force. Not just focusing on whether you can get a job. It’s about whether you can earn a high salary. Find a job in a high-growth field. Singapore recently introduced a four-day work week. That is, 3 days off per week. It is planned to start from December 1 this year. Employees can submit to their employer. Four day week job application. Increase telecommuting days. Or adjust working hours and other flexible working requirements. In line with the international trend of retaining talents. Singapore will also be the first in Asia. Countries with four-day working week. Singapore has the largest aging population in the world. one of the fastest countries. But Singapore’s pension system. Ranked 7th in the global ranking. Ranked first in Asia for 15 consecutive years. The Singapore government uses the "provident fund system" to. To protect the elderly. The financial burden of life after retirement. This provident fund is actually. Commonly known as pension pension. is a compulsory savings plan. All citizens and permanent residents. Just start working. A certain percentage of wages must be allocated. Go to this provident fund account. Employees under 55 years old. 20% self-withdrawal. Employer pays 17%.

Contribution ratio. It is decided based on age. For all Singapore employees. All adopt the same standard. Such a system. Let all the silver-haired people. You will be protected after retirement. Singapore’s Provident Fund Operation Model. Whether it is asset allocation. Or investment effect. are relatively diverse and stable. To ensure that the provident fund system can continue. The Singaporean government has also shown determination. Extend the retirement age year by year. Protecting the employment rights of senior citizens. It also eases social labor, medical. financial pressure etc. Mandatory savings and mandatory postponement of retirement age. This is difficult to do in a democratic country. And actually Singapore. It's not as good as it seems. After all, you still probably. Because if you evaluate the government, you will lose all benefits. As for the above benefits. In an authoritarian country? Surely it can be done, right? So has the CCP done it? Next, let’s talk about the occupied areas. Talking about Zhou Jinbo, Representative. Of the National People’s Congress. In this year’s two sessions. Recommended for 65 year olds. Establish a "universal basic pension system" I hope it can make people who have never worked. Have not paid social security. Elderly people who cannot enjoy basic pension security. can meet basic life needs. This may be the case in previous National People’s Congresses. The most valuable proposal. It has also become a hot topic in society. And Li Qiang, the Prime Minister of the Communist Party of China. In the government work report on March 5. "Urban and Rural Residents Basic Pension" The monthly minimum standard is increased by 20 yuan." There are now 170 million elderly people in occupied areas. Receive pension insurance for urban and rural residents. From 103 yuan per month. Increased to 123 yuan per month. The CCP is not claiming. Are we an inseparable piece? So can we lead both sides? The CCP’s pension system is a “dual-track system”. Within the system and outside the system. Civil servants and public institutions within the system. Participate in the pension insurance of government agencies. And institutions. No need to pay yourself. Pension is roughly. Linked to retirement salary. Those outside the system are corporate employees. It is a combination of social coordination and personal accounts. That is, companies and individuals. Sharing pension insurance premiums. But more migrant workers. and farmers are not covered in this set. In the pension security system. But for the sake of good-looking data. The CCP authorities have also targeted this group of people. Disburse basic pension to urban and rural residents. This is Prime Minister Li Qiang. The 170 million elderly people. Who increased their income by 20 yuan. The greater effect of this policy is. It allows the CCP to declare to the outside world. Pension security covers 96.8%. In January 2023.

There are 2.6 million job seekers including graduates. Apply for 37,100 entry-level government jobs. Among people aged 16 to 24 in occupied areas. Nearly 1 in 5 is unemployed. Alibaba, Tencent and other technology companies. have carried out large-scale layoffs. From August 2023. As the unemployment rate hits record highs. China stops publishing youth unemployment data. This has also led to Internet companies. blatant violation of the constitution. Launch of 996 work system. 9 a.m. every day. Work until 9pm. Work 6 days a week. Alibaba founder Jack Ma said. "It's a huge blessing to be able to do 996. Many companies, many people. Even if you want 996, there is no chance.”. I don’t know if he said anything against it. compared to singapore. Hope to be the first in Asia. Countries with 4-day working week. The CCP has become the world’s first. A place with a 12-hour work day. Sure enough, once again we are far ahead. Compulsory education in occupied areas. It is 6 years of elementary school plus 3 years of junior high school. No more than 50% from junior high school to high school. These students. Will attend vocational and technical schools. Less than half of the remaining students. Entering 3 years of high school studies. Enter university by taking the National College Entrance Examination. Admission to universities in occupied areas. It is not a "nationally consistent" scoring standard. but based on student. Ranking of "College Entrance Examination" scores. In each province. Some students in educational resources. Study in a relatively high province. But I will do it so that it will be easier for me. To get into a prestigious school. So I tried every means. Go to a province with lower admission standards. Go take the college entrance examination. Exploiting the Loophole of “Provincial Differences”. The same score in different provinces. there is a huge difference. Last year, Xi'an City, Shaanxi Province. There are over ten thousand people. Protest gathered at the Provincial Department of Education. Shouting "Xi'an babies want to go to school" The cause is Xi'an City. In the high school entrance examination. A large number of students from Henan appear. Come to Xi'an to grab a spot. As a result, many local students in Xi'an failed to pass the exam. Parents of these Xi'an students took to the streets. Questioning the exam qualifications of these Henan students. Protest against entrance examination methods and systems. create artificial inequalities. In addition to regional inequality. There is also status inequality. Studying in college in occupied areas. It’s not about taking the college entrance examination. As long as the score meets the standard. Many universities allocate quotas. Even if the score is up to standard. Even if your identity and qualifications are not met,. You will still be rejected. In a communist society. "Class inheritance" always exists. The Communist Party has always emphasized. I am a hero and a good man. I'm a reactionary bastard.

In such a "hereditary" society. Some red second and third generation people. With your dad or your grandpa. You can get into Peking University with low scores. Study in these famous schools like Tsinghua University. Different from bilingual education in Singapore. The occupied areas are being de-Englishized. 2023. Xi'an Jiaotong University issued a notice. He expressed that he would no longer submit the results. Of CET-4 and CET-6. Linked to undergraduate graduation and bachelor's degree. Back in 2020. Education Department of the Communist Party of China. It is prohibited for elementary and junior high schools. Use textbooks from overseas. A government adviser suggested. English should be stopped in the college entrance examination. Some professors who did not want to be named said. Universities also do not encourage. Original English and translated books. especially in the news. and more sensitive subjects such as constitutional studies. Communist Party’s Orthodox Theory. is replacing english. Elementary, middle and high school. Being asked to read. Xi Jinping’s new era. New textbooks on socialist thought with Chinese characteristics. Every student is required to. Attend a class once a week. Unlike before, everyone had a copy of Mao’s Quotations. Different approaches but similar results. Finally we talk about. Housing development in occupied areas. before reform and opening up. The occupied areas implement unit-based. Welfare housing allocation system. The government is fully responsible for. Responsibility for housing urban residents. The central government formulates plans to allocate funds. Local governments and units use this money. Build housing and distribute it to employees. housing benefit distribution system. resulting in a lack of housing and small living space. Housing is of poor quality and lacks maintenance. Issues such as unfair housing distribution. before housing reform. Urban housing area per capita. Only 3.8 square meters. That’s more than 1 square meter for us. About the same size as your toilet. There is simply no way to meet everyone’s housing needs. from the 20th century. Beginning in the late 1970s. The CCP implements housing marketization. Socialization and Monetization Reform. After more than 20 years of housing system reform,. There are still many problems in the housing sector. Take Peking as an example. In 2000. Price per square meter. About 2800 RMB. In 2020. It has risen to. 58,000 per square meter. Complete marketization. Can reap economic dividends. There are only powerful people and financial groups. After all, people who have money to buy a house. Mostly this group of people. It turned out that ordinary families who could not afford a house. It will be even harder to buy a house. Supporting affordable housing. That is our national residence in Taiwan. Mainly to protect grassroots officials.

But will their lives be difficult? Low-rent housing is provided by the government. Room type at 1/3 the price. Its basic condition is that citizens. But you still need to have some connections with the government. At least there's someone at home. You can only get it in the civil service system. Workers and migrant workers whose lives are more difficult. They are never guaranteed. This kind of housing system. For families with real housing difficulties. causing greater injustice. These officials and civil servants. Rent a house from the government. The phenomenon of subletting to migrant workers with extra money. This is also common in occupied areas. However, the government has always implemented this policy. viewed as socialist benefits. Give it to the system. No corrupt grassroots civil servants. so that these people can enjoy. The so-called dividends of communism. Singapore and China. Both are authoritarian systems. But what Singapore implements is. benevolent authoritarianism. More like a perfect communist system. assumes the responsibility of comprehensively. Taking care of the people. Education, medical care, residence, elderly care. Responsibilities required in all aspects of life. but. Of course, benevolent despotism is also despotism. Also has fatal flaws. The first is that benevolent dictators are not common. The second is that there is no guarantee that. They will always do good things. Third, it is difficult to guarantee their heirs. will have the same ability to continue doing good deeds. What the CCP implements is. socialism with Chinese characteristics. Socialism can only be implemented within the party. They divided society into three, six, and nine grades. Leaders, officials, citizens, farmers. Distribute social resources from most to least. More focus on protection. The privileged class of leaders and officials. basic social security. It is difficult to cover many farmers. Li Keqiang before his death. I once said at a press conference. China has 600 million low- and middle-income people. The monthly income is only about 1,000 yuan. There are also 67 million people living in extreme poverty. Because today’s Holy. Talk about eradicating poverty. Therefore, it was not included in the report. It’s different from a democratic country. The well-being of people in authoritarian countries. completely in the hands of the head of state. And in a democratic country. People's well-being may be slow to improve. But if an official or political party does something wrong. People can use their votes to replace them. Make sure the government is on the right path. However, authoritarian countries can only use. The bloody way of revolution has changed. alright. So that’s it for this episode. if you like. Welcome to click subscribe and share it for us. If you have capable friends. You can also join our membership. Or click Super Thank You. In addition, our DC group is now open. Everyone is welcome to come in and chat.

My study notes. See you next time. ByeBye.

====

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wtlFgc9O-OY

雖然成熟的民主國家

在經濟上的整體表現

會優於專制國家

但少數的專制國家

會把注意力集中在經濟增長上面

它們在發展方面

超越了新興的民主國家

例如朴正熙領導下的韓國

鄧小平領導下的中共

以及李光耀領導下的新加坡之類的

新加坡跟中共

一樣都是威權體制

但是有什麼不一樣呢？

今天就我們來聊聊新加坡跟中共的專制有什麼不同吧！

#臺灣 #台灣 #中華民國 #中國 #新加坡 #專制 #中共 #南韓 #專制政府 #民主 #國家 #體制 #李光耀 #李明龍 #溫家寶 #習近平 #江澤明 #中共專制 #新加坡專制 #民生 #政策 #國策

📍特別感謝每位默默支持我們的朋友們，你們給的不只是錢財，更是一段友誼 當你像小蝦米一樣對抗大鯨魚，當你的敵人遠比你想像中強大的時候 發現了這個世界，其實有一群未曾相識卻力挺你的朋友，我想，這就是世界上最美好的感覺了
